rulings.Case Title: DANIEL MONTOYA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 14, 2008, line 1, DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE DUE TO DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO OBTAIN RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT HE IS UNABLE TO PROVE HIS CASE BASED ON EVIDENCE FROM OTHER SOURCES. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD DONG et al VS. HARRY WOO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, OCTOBER 14, 2008. LINE 11. PLAINTIFFS ROLAND DONG, JEANETTE CORDERO, ANGELA POLAND AND DESIREE POLAND'S Motion To File First Amended Complaint. A hearing is required for plaintiff to delineate the facts that have "come to light based upon... conduct during the pendency of the instant action." =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: VIRGINIA BALDOVIESO VS. KEN WOO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, OCTOBER 14, 2008. LINE 12. DEFENDANT CITIMORTGAGE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to explain why plaintiff did not discover the facts related to the 2004 loan when it seems apparent that no work was being done on the additional bedroom. Plaintiff cannot ignore the facts set forth in the documents attached to the original complaint related to the receipt of the notice of right to cancel. Plaintiff must plead facts that establish a basis for Woo being defendant's agent, not plaintiff's agent. Plaintiff must allege how, when, where and by what means defendant committed constructive fraud. =(302/PJM/VC)
